message:
test(time): vDSO vs raw-syscall parity for clock_gettime (bd-j946)

Review of cod's vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2) found a coverage gap:
the existing vdso_fastpath_snapshot + hit-counter tests prove the
fastpath is *invoked* but never verify it *agrees* with the syscall
reference — a silent clock_id mapping or timespec-layout bug would
pass the existing tests.

Add two parity tests bracketing the vDSO-preferring time_abi call
between two raw_syscall::sys_clock_gettime samples:

clock_realtime_vdso_agrees_with_raw_syscall
  Uses raw_syscall as ground truth (bypasses libc to avoid any
  ABI-symbol interception). Allows 50ms NTP slack on CLOCK_REALTIME.

clock_monotonic_vdso_agrees_with_raw_syscall
  CLOCK_MONOTONIC is tight — any divergence between vDSO and syscall
  paths would violate the shared kernel timekeeper invariant.

The two planned CLOCK_PROCESS/THREAD_CPUTIME_ID counter tests were
dropped: a global hit counter can't be asserted under Cargo's parallel
test execution (concurrent vDSO-supported clock calls from other tests
race the before/after snapshot).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-dfe", "title": "EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)", "description": "Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T02:13:43.636260651Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T02:13:36.812282675Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-1az", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-1tx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-1wy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-29r", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-31i", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-ju7", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-dfe", "depends_on_id": "bd-p5i", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1083, "issue_id": "bd-dfe", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"}, {"id": 1084, "issue_id": "bd-dfe", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "CobaltCompass: All 7 blockers CLOSED. check_module_inventory.sh (68/68 in sync), check_module_wiring.sh, check_snapshot_coverage.sh (68/68 covered), snapshot_gate.sh, perf_gate.sh all passing. Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:13:43Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-dg3r","title":"syscall: io_uring sqpoll veneer parity","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:50.911253411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:57.610277502Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-di5w", "title": "Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry", "description": "## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic — streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:17:17.216902967Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T02:45:57.935547570Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T02:45:57.935252511Z", "close_reason": "Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["io-internal", "native", "stub-elimination"], "comments": [{"id": 1361, "issue_id": "bd-di5w", "author": "ubuntu", "text": "Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-e3y8","title":"wordexp parity: quote-aware badchar and command-substitution semantics","description":"Background:
- `wordexp` is marked Implemented, but current parsing is too naive about quoting/escaping context.
- Host glibc accepts quoted and escaped metacharacters literally and only rejects true unquoted shell separators.

Goal:
- Bring the native `wordexp` implementation into parity with host glibc for a bounded shell-lexing slice.

Deliverables:
1) Quote-aware/escape-aware bad-character validation for `wordexp`.
2) Quote-aware command-substitution detection so `WRDE_NOCMD` only rejects real command substitution.
3) Host-parity ABI tests for quoted semicolons, escaped separators, quoted command substitution literals, and bare bad-char cases.

Verification:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test wordexp_ -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:01:59.732205136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.852185122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:09:46.850227737Z","close_reason":"Match glibc wordexp quote/escape badchar and WRDE_NOCMD semantics with host-parity tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-j946","title":"vDSO gettime fastpath: add parity + fallback coverage","description":"Review of cod's recent vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2/time_abi.rs) found coverage gaps in tests: (1) vdso_fastpath_snapshot and hit-counter tests confirm the fastpath is HIT but never verify the fastpath AGREES with the syscall fallback — a silent divergence (e.g., wrong clock_id mapping) would pass the existing tests. (2) No test verifies that CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID (not vDSO-exported) does NOT increment vdso_clock_gettime_hits. (3) No test for EINVAL propagation when vdso_clock_gettime rejects an unsupported clock. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs. Use raw_syscall::sys_clock_gettime as ground-truth reference to avoid libc-symbol interception issues.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:29.950988891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:35.953433160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T03:26:23.410891503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T03:29:49.488009800Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T03:29:49.486025815Z","close_reason":"Added raw get_mempolicy veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall tests and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-ju7", "title": "Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)", "description": "Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "DustyPuma", "created_at": "2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-ju7", "depends_on_id": "bd-1tx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-ju7", "depends_on_id": "bd-1wy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1101, "issue_id": "bd-ju7", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)", "created_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jucr","title":"Execute stdio_file_ops fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade stdio_file_ops conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:56:21.902983257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T01:01:34.108283648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T01:01:34.104663411Z","close_reason":"Upgraded stdio_file_ops conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
